---

A weapon etched with a _deathdrinking_ rune shimmers with dark purple energy. When held by a living creature, the weapon causes twinges of hunger to manifest. 

While holding a _deathdrinking weapon_, you gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) damage and [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects. When you critically hit a creature with a _deathdrinking weapon_, you inflict an additional 1d6 points of positive or negative damage to the creature— whichever type of damage would harm the creature. You also gain the following reaction when wielding a _deathdrinking weapon_.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** you kill or destroy a creature with the _deathdrinking weapon_;  **Effect** If the creature you killed was living, you gain a +1 item bonus to attack and damage rolls for 10 minutes. If the creature you destroyed was [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), you gain a number of temporary HP equal to twice your level for 10 minutes.

</column>

**Source** [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

As with [_energy-resistant_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=283) runes, these runes convey protective forces from the Elemental Planes. You gain 5 resistance to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72). The crafter chooses the damage type when creating the rune. Multiple _energy-absorbing runes_ can be etched onto a suit of armor; rather than using only the highest-level effect, each must provide resistance to a different type. When an _energy-absorbing rune_ provides resistance to damage from a foe's attack or ability, the armor itself appears to become infused with that energy, be it dripping with acid, riming over in frost, crackling with lightning, or flickering with flames. This effect does not inflict damage itself and ends at the start of your next turn, or as soon as you take the following reaction.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** Your armor is infused with energy and you take damage from a melee Strike;  **Effect** You unleash the infused energy against the triggering melee Strike, inflicting 2d6 damage of that energy type to the creature who made the melee Strike.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Curtain Call Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1782)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 78

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blue velvet cloak is cut from the remains of a curtain from a destroyed theater. When worn, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks while acting, orating, or singing, as the cloak makes minor motions to accentuate your performance.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You take a bow, spread your arms, and the cloak casts 4th-level [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) centered on a corner of your space. This darkness doesn't impede your vision. While the darkness persists, it emits noise like the applauding of a moderately sized crowd.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Curtain Call Cloak

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs=""
>
[Phantom Shroud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1783)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 78

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale blue cloak is wispy, thin, and cold to the touch. When worn, the cloak turns your hands pale and translucent, imbuing you with the dangerous touch of a [ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51). This effect doesn't impede the normal use of your hands. You also gain an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack that deals 3d6 negative damage with the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), and [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) traits. You gain the benefits of a [_+2 weapon potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=281) rune with these unarmed attacks (granting a +2 item bonus to your attack rolls).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You fold the cloak around yourself, and it casts [_ethereal jaunt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=105) on you. You can Sustain the activation for up to 10 minutes. When the activation ends, you return to material form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phantom Shroud

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Spectral Opera Glasses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1784)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 78

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These bulky opera glasses are attached to a rod for ease of use. When held up to the eyes, which requires a free hand, you can see up to four times further than normal, and the glasses provide you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 hour, anyone who looks through the glasses can see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures and objects, and sees creatures with the [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) trait as solid and substantial (rather than ghostly).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectral Opera Glasses

May contain spoilers from Blood Lords **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 78 **Price** 1,600 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands or mounted **Bulk** 3 --- This drum-shaped metal floodlight has a metal handle on each side and a housing at its base to be slotted into a stand or pedestal. A _splendid floodlight_ has a primary function as well as several secondary functions available by rotating through a series of translucent sheets called gels. A _splendid floodlight_ comes with the four gels described below, but other gels exist with different abilities. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Requirements** The _splendid floodlight's_ light is off; **Effect** You flip a switch and the _splendid floodlight_ emits bright magical light in a 120-foot cone that hangs in the air and remains in place even if you move the floodlight. If the cone passes through an area of magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) or targets a creature affected by magical darkness, the splendid floodlight attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the darkness. Creatures in the cone who are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) reduce the value of their frightened condition by 1 at the beginning of each turn they start in the area of the cone. The light remains in place until you Interact to flip the switch again to extinguish the floodlight. If you haven't done so after an hour, the switch flips on its own and the floodlight turns off. **Activate** Interact; **Requirements** The _splendid floodlight's_ light is on; **Effect** You rotate one of the following gels into position in the floodlight or rotate a gel out. You can do this even if the _splendid floodlight_ has been moved from its position when the light was created. You can have up to two gels in place at once; if you rotate in a third, another of your choice rotates out.
  • **Red Gel** The light lends an air of gravitas to all creatures illuminated by it. Creatures in the light gain a +2 item bonus to Charisma-based skill checks.
  • **Yellow Gel** [Invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures and objects are visible while in the light, and creatures with the [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) trait appear substantial rather than ghostly.
  • **Green Gel** All creatures illuminated by the light appear to be wearing exceptionally stylish clothing with elegantly coiffed hair styles.
  • **Blue Gel** [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures in the light appear as they did when alive, and living creatures in the light appear decayed, like an undead creature.
name: Splendid Floodlight

<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 79

**Price** 23,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56)_ has a thin, delicate blade that absorbs light.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Interact ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** one per day;   **Effect** You make a melee Strike with _shadow's heart_ and teleport to an open space you can see within 100 feet. If the attack hits, you also leave behind a shadowy, illusory duplicate of yourself that attacks the same target for up to 3 rounds. The illusion has 1 action per round, which it takes on the turn it's created and on your turn each round. It uses this action to move up to 30 feet to a square adjacent to the target and (if the target is adjacent) to attack the target with a +28 attack modifier, dealing 3d6+16 slashing damage. The illusion has an AC of 35 and is immediately destroyed by any attack that deals damage or requires a saving throw.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadow's Heart

>
[Umbraex Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1787)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 79

**Price** 34,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Like the rest of its form, the eyes of the legendary umbraex darvakka (page 83) collapse into ashes when the creature is destroyed. Rarely, when the umbraex wishes to impart information beyond its ashen reconstitution, it ejects one of its eyes at the moment of destruction. This large orb hardens into an obsidian-like substance that swirls with captivated motes of darkness deep within. While you hold the _umbraex eye_, you have [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) (imprecise) 120 feet.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command, Interact);  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Name a person, event, or location and the _umbraex eye_ shows 1 minute of relevant memory the umbraex witnessed firsthand. The events are displayed as clear images in the eye and accompanied by sound. If the umbraex has no relevant memories, the eye merely flickers with shadows.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Umbraex Eye

This bottle of delicately sweet ice wine has the properties of [alcohol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=622). Made exclusively from grapes frozen in the Mana Wastes' erratic surge storms, Alkenstar ice wine finds a ready market among Geb, though [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) are still immune to the drug's listed effects.

 The listed price is for a mediocre vintage, but finer vintages are higher level and commensurately more expensive.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Plated Duster](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1789)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 106

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Characters from Alkenstar have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These loose-fitting long coats of canvas or leather are lined with metal plates, offering wearers respite from Alkenstar's dust storms and smog stains as well as modest protection against bullets and knives. While these dusters can't turn away gunfire entirely, their utility, affordability, and comfort ensure their popularity, especially among the city's shieldmarshals, who have adopted them as a sort of unofficial uniform. A plated duster can be donned with 2 Interact actions or as part of donning light armor. When worn with light armor from the cloth, leather, or chain groups, a plated duster increases the armor's item bonus to AC by 1, worsens the armor's check penalty by 1, reduces the armor's Dex cap by 1, increases the Strength score required to ignore the armor check penalty and Speed penalty by 2, adds the [noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=205) trait, and changes the armor's group to [composite](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=2). This also makes the armor one step heavier (from light to medium), and you use the proficiency bonus appropriate to this adjusted armor type. You can't use a plated duster alongside an [armored skirt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=514) or any other item that adjusts an armor's statistics.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Plated Duster

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 177

**Price** 18 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Geb have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This simple charm is made from compacted grave dirt infused with bone dust. A [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) spell that's empowered by this catalyst can reach faraway targets. If the _harm_ spell is cast with 1 action, its range is 30 feet; if it's cast with 2 actions, its range is 60 feet. This has no effect on the three-action area version of _harm_, though in most cases, you don't have enough actions to Activate the token and cast a three-action _harm_ anyway.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grave Token

Blood offered from a willing donor was taken directly from the vein and stored in an ornate vial that keeps it as pure and red as the day it was extracted. When this catalyst is added to a [_vampiric touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) spell of up to 4th level, instead of gaining temporary Hit Points based on the damage dealt, you recover half of the damage dealt as Hit Points.

</traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vulture's Wing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1796)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 178

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Geb have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This fan of vulture feathers scatters on an unnatural gust of wind. If this catalyst is used to cast a [_ray of enfeeblement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) spell that has been heightened to at least 5th level, instead of targeting a single creature, you cast the spell in a 30-foot-area cone. You don't make a spell attack roll, instead affecting all creatures within the area with the effect the spell would normally have on a hit. This means creatures within the area must attempt a Fortitude save to determine whether they're [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Carrion Cask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1797)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 178

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Geb have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This stylized, palm-sized box contains a black, ooze-like substance that can easily devour a corpse in moments, breaking the body down into a necromantic sludge.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You feed a corpse of a Small-sized or larger creature to the cask, little by little. The activation requires a three-action activity for a Small or Medium creature, a 1-minute activity for a Larger creature, and a 10-minute activity for a Huge creature. A Gargantuan creature is too large for the cask to devour.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The _carrion cask_ has consumed a corpse since the last time it was activated; **Effect** You release the sludge from the _carrion cask's_ last meal in a pulse of necromantic energy. Creatures in a 30-foot cone take 6d6 negative damage, with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Carrion Cask

The bones of small animals decorate the cover of this tome, creating a pattern that resembles a gross perversion of [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) holy symbol.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) minion you summoned or created using a spell prepared from this grimoire takes damage that would bring it to 0 Hit Points;  **Effect** You call out and demand the undead to remain, reaching out with tendrils of negative energy that preserves your minion so it might continue to serve you. Expend a spell slot in which you've prepared a [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) spell to restore 1d8 Hit Points to your undead minion per level of the expended spell slot, before applying the damage. If this prevents the minion from being reduced to 0 Hit Points, it isn't destroyed.

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 178 **Price** 825 gp **Bulk** L when not activated **Access** Characters from Geb have access to this item. --- This small square of dark blue fabric appears to be painted with a depiction of the night sky, shifting each day to match the sky above Geb the previous night. **Activate** Interact; **Effect** The fabric unfolds into a dark palanquin large enough to comfortably hold two Medium creatures. Four spectral bearers appear at the corners of the palanquin, capable of carrying it effortlessly over most types of terrain as long as the terrain is relatively flat and devoid of hazards. Spectral bearers have the statistics of an [unseen servant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1), except they're strong enough to carry the palanquin and its occupants. They move generally along a basic set of directions you indicate upon activation, which can include turns onto various streets, but they don't take other creatures' feelings into account and can be inconsiderate to passersby in an inhabited area as they relentlessly move you forward. The bearers move slowly but steadily along their simple directions. While the palanquin is perfectly suitable for overland travel, the bearers' imprecise movements and the requirement to reactivate the palanquin to change destinations make it unsuited for a combat encounter or other situation where seconds and precise movements count.
The palanquin's interior ceiling is painted with the same night sky pattern as the cloth, and dark curtains hang over the windows. The palanquin protects its occupants from sunlight and [severe heat and cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) (though not extreme or incredible heat or cold) as well as blocks most non-damaging forms of weather. The palanquin is only as sturdy as a standard wooden carriage—if it would be destroyed, it instantly reverts to a square of fabric and can't be Activated again for 1 month.
You can return the palanquin to its original shape by using an Interact action to command the spectral bearers to collapse it. They disappear the moment it returns to its fabric form.
name: Palanquin of Night

    level="1"

>
[Sunflower Censer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1800)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 178

**Price** 480 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gold-and-ivory incense burner always emits a pleasant, floral-scented smoke without any need to be refilled. It has a chain that can be easily affixed to a belt or bag, allowing it to be worn without a free hand. As long as the censer is burning, the bearer benefits from the [_undetectable alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=348) spell. They also don't appear [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) to [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) spells or abilities, or to senses such as [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), unless those abilities successfully [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the censer. The censer can be lit or extinguished with an Interact action.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Smoke bellows from the censer with the effects of an [_obscuring mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) centered on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sunflower Censer

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 178 **Price** 500 gp **Bulk** L **Access** Characters from Geb have access to this item. --- A spirited debate persists among scholars on whether the eponymous tyrant of this grimoire actually refers to Tar-Baphon, the necromancer Geb, or even the goddess [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19) herself. This grimoire appears at first to be a series of writs that makes arrogant demands of the reader, those around them, and the universe. Anyone who gives these writings more than a cursory look realizes the writs hold spells much like any other grimoire, with the [_animate dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=666) spell being particularly prominent. _Tyrant's writs_ grants you the ability to demand more from the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) you animate. **Activate** envision ([metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell to cast an _animate dead_ spell prepared with _tyrant's writs_, you can choose one of the following additional benefits to grant the summoned undead.
  • **Bloody** The undead gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) equal to its level.
  • **Explosive** When it reaches 0 Hit Points, the undead explodes, dealing 4d6 fire damage to adjacent creatures, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save using the _animate dead_ spell's DC. As normal, since the [_final sacrifice_](/Spells.aspx?ID=689) spell doesn't reduce the target to 0 Hit Points, it doesn't cause this explosion.
  • **Necrotic Speed** The undead gains a +10-foot status bonus to all its Speeds.
  • **Rotten** Any living creature who starts its turn adjacent to the undead who isn't at full Hit Points takes 1d6 poison damage.
name: Tyrant's Writ

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Condensed Mana](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1802)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 234

**Price** 69 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Condensed mana is a crystal vial filled with concentrated miasma from the Mana Wastes. The colors shift abruptly and unpredictably within and the opening of the bottle is sealed tighter than any other potion or alchemical item, emphasizing its dangers.

 On a successful Strike, the creature takes 1 force damage. Then, roll on the [Wellspring Surge table](/Rules.aspx?ID=1576). The result affects all creatures within the emanation indiscriminately. For the purpose of determining the effects of the surge, use a spell level of 4 and a DC of 23. The person Striking with this vial is considered to be the caster for the purposes of determining effects on the Mana Surge table. The emanation is centered on the target's space, and both the caster and the target of the wellspring surge are the target of the condensed mana. On a failed Strike, the surge only has an effect if it would normally affect an area; if you roll an effect that depends on a caster or a target, nothing happens. If the effect normally doesn't have a DC, creatures affected by the surge or within the surge's area must attempt a DC 23 Reflex save instead. On a critical failure on any saving throw against condensed mana, the creature is stunned 1 by the wild energies of the surge.

 Sometimes a wellspring surge's effects make it difficult to determine exactly who is affected. The GM makes the determination, basing it on creatures directly affected; for example, while many creatures can hear the music from the surge “strike up the band,” only the creature emanating the music is directly affected by it.

**Craft Requirements** You must be in or adjacent to an area of Mana Wastes miasma.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Condensed Mana

markdown:
**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 304 **Price** 3,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand --- A _cube of force_ is an enchanted cube that measures an inch across. While made from any hard material, the sides of a _cube of force_ are decorated so that they can be distinguished by touch. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You hold the cube aloft and depress one of its six faces for several seconds. The cube creates six walls around you, creating a cube 10 feet on each side centered on you (typically centered on the top of your space if you're a Medium creature). If a creature or object overlaps any of these walls, that face of the cube doesn't appear; this also means if you're Huge or larger, the activation has no effect, as your space is larger than the cube would be. The duration and effect of the six walls depends on which face of the cube you press when you Activate the cube, as seen on Table 5: Cube Effects (below). Pressing the sixth cube face with a simple Interact action Dismisses the effect; doing so isn't an activation and thus doesn't count against the cube's frequency. The walls have the AC, Hit Points, and Hardness of a [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=363). ## Cube Effects
**Cube Face****Duration****Effect**
1 30 minutes The walls keep out gas and wind, attempting to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) such effects that cross the threshold with a +20 counteract modifier but allowing all other things through.
210 minutesThe walls block nonliving matter from crossing; a nonliving creature must succeed at a DC 30 Will save to cross the threshold, wasting their move action on a failure, and unattended objects can't cross. Non-magical ranged attacks can't cross either and instead attack the wall. A living creature can bring attended objects across the walls without needing to attempt a saving throw.
310 minutesThe walls block living matter from crossing; a living creature must succeed at a DC 30 Will save to cross the threshold, wasting their move action on a failure.
45 minutesThe walls keep out magical effects that cross the threshold, attempting to counteract magical effects with a +20 counteract modifier, though they don't prevent a creature with magical items or effects from entering.
51 minuteThe walls block everything, with the effects of [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=363).
6—The cube is Dismissed (this takes only an Interact action and isn't an activation).
name: Cube of Force

A _Lesser Cube of Nex_ acts as a _[cube of force](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1803)_, but the effect lasts until you press the sixth face to Dismiss it, and the activation can be used once per hour. You must first Dismiss any previous activation before you use it again. Additionally, each _Lesser Cube of Nex_ is attuned to a single school of magic. Within 5 feet of the _Lesser Cube_, all magic from that school is suppressed or impossible to cast, with the effects of a 10th-level [_antimagic field_](/Spells.aspx?ID=14) spell. While there are eight _Lesser Cubes of Nex_, each of the artifacts is unique, as there's only one _Lesser Cube of Nex_ for each school of magic.

[Abraxas](/Deities.aspx?ID=71) teaches that the true heart is impenetrable, dedicated only to knowledge. This tattoo of a venom-soaked heart provides a +1 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You're about to attempt a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect or an effect that would make you [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6);  **Effect** Abraxas envelops your heart in the shadow of his secret, granting you a +2 status bonus on your saving throw against the triggering effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silent Heart

[Abraxas](/Deities.aspx?ID=71) teaches that an eye for an eye is the basis of law. This tattoo of a coiled viper provides a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** Abraxas transforms the tattoo into a weapon of your vengeance. The _vengeful arm_ crawls into your hand and becomes a [retribution axe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=393). You can use this activation again while holding the axe to revert the vengeful arm to a tattoo. Like other _retribution axes_, you can etch fundamental runes into the _vengeful arm's_ _retribution axe_ to increase its potency or to add [_striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Secrets must be unraveled, no matter how painful. This tattoo of braided tongues provides a +1 item bonus to Deception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** [Abraxas](/Deities.aspx?ID=71) fills your tongue with venom, causing your deceptions to poison your foes. You use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=45), [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48), or [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47). Choose one target against whom your Deception check succeeded; that target takes 2d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Goo Grenade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1811)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 306

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Nex have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A goo grenade is a flask filled with a fast-growing, short-lived alchemical ooze. When you hit a creature with a goo grenade, that creature takes [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and a circumstance penalty to its Speeds from the clinging goo.

 The target can end any penalties, conditions, and persistent damage caused by the bomb by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or spending a total of 3 Interact actions to pry themselves free of the ooze. These Interact actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

</traits>

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The target takes 1d4 acid damage, 1 persistent acid damage, and 1 acid splash damage. They take a –5-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds. The Escape DC is 15.

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The target takes 2d4 acid damage, 2 persistent acid damage, and 2 acid splash damage. They take a –5-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds. The Escape DC is 17.

>
Goo Grenade (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The target takes 3d4 acid damage, 3 persistent acid damage, and 3 acid splash damage. They take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds. The Escape DC is 26.

A goo grenade is a flask filled with a fast-growing, short-lived alchemical ooze. When you hit a creature with a goo grenade, that creature takes [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and a circumstance penalty to its Speeds from the clinging goo.

 The target can end any penalties, conditions, and persistent damage caused by the bomb by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or spending a total of 3 Interact actions to pry themselves free of the ooze. These Interact actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 25"
    pfs=""
>
[Passage Pane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1814)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pane of this large circular mirror has a diameter of 8 feet and a gray cast. The mirror reflects its surroundings in muted colors, and any living creatures that look into the mirror see themselves as preserved corpses with sallow skin, sunken cheeks, and a vacant expression. The mirror is mounted in a bone frame intricately carved with imagery of corvids, keys, and winding roads. These roads represent the Dead Roads, the secret back routes between the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) and all other planes that are under the purview of the [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83) usher [Barzahk the Passage](/Deities.aspx?ID=138) and their followers.

 _Passage panes_ are created by incredibly powerful priests of Barzahk using mirrors that have reflected Barzahk during their travels from when Barzahk passes through mortal realms. Through prayer, powerful magic, and exceptional crafting skill, these mirrors form a spiritual connection to the Dead Roads, which can be harnessed to open a gate to the Boneyard.

 To utilize a _passage pane_, the mirror must be securely mounted on a solid surface. Once mounted, a user must bond with the _passage pane_ by gazing beyond their dead visage into the Dead Roads. Bonding takes patience and can only be accomplished by a living creature who has at least master proficiency in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) and who succeeds at a DC 40 Religion check. Attempting to bond with the mirror in this way takes 1 hour. Once a creature is bonded with the mirror, they can use the passage pane to open a portal to a random point along the Dead Roads. A creature with knowledge of a location in the Boneyard can force the mirror to lock onto that location, allowing more pointed travel. Only one creature can be bonded with the mirror at a time. When a creature forms a bond with the mirror, any previous bonds are immediately severed. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) can't bond with the passage pane.

 When Barzahk themself walks the Dead Roads, a depiction of them appears in the frame's carvings. When Barzahk is depicted in this way, they can prevent the passage pane from Activating or alter the destination of the _passage pane_, if desired.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact; [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141))   **Requirements** The _passage pane_ is mounted firmly to a solid surface, and you're bonded with the _passage pane_; **Effect** You focus on a location in the Boneyard that you wish to travel to. You must have either seen or visited this location before or be able to describe the location in detail and its purpose or name, as appropriate. Attempt a DC 40 Religion check. On a success, the mirror locks onto your desired location, and you (and only you) see this location reflected in the mirror's pane.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** once per week;  **Requirements** The _passage pane_ is mounted firmly to a solid surface, and you're bonded with the _passage pane_; **Effect** You urge the mirror to open a rift to the Boneyard. Creatures can travel through this rift in either direction. If you've successfully forced the mirror to lock onto a specific location, the mirror's pane becomes a rift to the Boneyard that opens onto that location. If you haven't forced the mirror to lock onto a specific location, the mirror's pane becomes a rift to a random location on the Dead Roads. This rift remains open until you close it, up to a maximum of 24 hours. As long as the rift is open, the mirror is immovable, no one can form a bond with the mirror, and no one but you can Activate the mirror.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> envision, Interact ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156))   **Requirements** The _passage pane_ is an active rift to the Boneyard, and you're bonded with the _passage pane_; **Effect** You close the rift.

**Destruction** The passage pane shatters into thousands of pieces if Barzahk the Passage bonds with the mirror and wills its destruction.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Passage Pane

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 23"
    pfs=""
>
[Redsand Hourglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1815)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This platinum hourglass is filled with red sand, is decorated with imagery of roses and whippoorwills with tiny black onyx eyes, and has the ability to manipulate time. Once used by [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) herself, the _Redsand Hourglass_ was stolen from Pharasma's court a millennia ago. While the hourglass is in your possession, you're immune to the [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) conditions.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail a saving throw;  **Effect** You flip the hourglass upside down then immediately right it again, rewinding time by a few moments. Reroll the saving throw with a +4 circumstance bonus. You must use the results of this new saving throw.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You flip the hourglass upside down, unhinging you from the flow of time. You become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 minute. You can use this extra action only to Step, Stride, or Sustain a Spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You flip the hourglass sideways, pausing time for everyone but yourself. You cast [_time stop_](/Spells.aspx?ID=339).

**Destruction** The _Redsand Hourglass_ is destroyed if it's used within the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=21).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Redsand Hourglass

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Soulspark Candle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1816)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Consecration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=34" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick, white pillar candle has flecks of ash and bone mixed amid the wax. When lit, the candle sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) for 20 feet and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for a further 20 feet. The candle can't be snuffed by weather, water, or accident but can be purposefully snuffed through ritual prayer. The candle doesn't release heat and can't be used to start a fire. The candle is never exhausted, regardless of how long it burns.

 The area within the candle's light is a consecrated space. Worshippers of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) within this area gain a +3 status bonus to attack rolls, skill checks, saving throws, and Perception checks. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures and worshippers of [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19) within this area take a –3 status penalty to those checks.

 When an undead creature or a [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=88) comes within the light of a _soulspark candle_, the candle's flame turns from red to black, makes a loud crackling sound, and releases a flash of bright sparks. The candle continues to burn black and crackle for as long as a haunt or undead remains in the area.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You light the candle.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;    **Requirements** The candle is lit; **Effect** You put out the candle with ritual prayers to Pharasma.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100))   **Requirements** The candle is lit, and an undead is within the light's area; **Effect** You command the candle to illuminate the dead. All undead creatures in the area are limned in blue, heatless fire for as long as they remain in the area, plus 1 minute after they leave the area or the light goes out. Visible undead can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while limned in these flames. [Invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) undead are concealed while limned in these flames, rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). Haunts are unaffected by this light.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72))   **Requirements** The candle is lit, and an undead or haunt is within the light's area; **Effect** You command the candle to destroy the undead. The candle's flame erupts into a mass of fire that burns away undead and haunts. Any undead within the candle's light takes 20d8 fire damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Pharasma empowers these flames, allowing them to ignore fire resistance or immunity. Any haunts in the area are instead subject to Pharasma's banishing light. The candle attempts a check to disable each haunt with a +35 modifier. This check applies to any skills applicable to disable the haunt and always has legendary proficiency. Once the candle is Activated in this way, its light immediately goes out, and the candle can't be relit for 1 hour.

**Destruction** If a _soulspark candle_ is lit by an undead of at least 20th level that worships Urgathoa, the candle burns down as if it were a normal candle over the course of 48 hours. At the end of this time, if the candle is still burning, it burns away completely and is destroyed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Soulspark Candle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs=""
>
[Spiral Athame](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1817)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Blood Lords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #186: Ghost King's Rage](/Sources.aspx?ID=189) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pommel of this +4 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [disruptive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=294) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) [high-grade silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) is a black glass orb that contains a tiny blue comet that spirals through the orb's interior. Strikes with the dagger deal an additional 1d8 positive damage to worshippers of [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19) and to anyone who has ever created or [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature.

 While the dagger is in your possession, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) checks and to [Midwifery Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks. If you're a worshipper of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), you gain [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) with a range of 60 feet while holding the dagger. If you're undead or a worshipper of Urgathoa, you're [stupefied 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) while holding the dagger.

 Despite its utility in combat, the _Spiral Athame_ is intended as a ritual implement, meant to bless newborns and protect them from the predations of undead. If you use the _Spiral Athame_ to cut the umbilical cord of a newly born creature, that creature gains resistance to negative damage equal to their level (minimum 1) and gains a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects and [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117). These bonuses are permanent. Additionally, that creature can never become an undead creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, interact ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117))    **Effect** You point the _Spiral Athame_ at a corpse within 30 feet, shielding it from corruption. The _Spiral Athame_ casts 5th-level [_gentle repose_](/Spells.aspx?ID=131) on the corpse.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, interact ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117))    **Effect** You point the _Spiral Athame_ to the sky and pray, warding creatures against undead. This has the effect of 3rd-level [_circle of protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=38), except it wards against undead creatures rather than creatures of a specified alignment, and the emanation is centered on the _Spiral Athame_.

**Destruction** If an undead creature uses the _Spiral Athame_ to slay a [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83) usher while within the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15), the _Spiral Athame's_ pommel shatters and the _Spiral Athame_ becomes a mundane, high-grade silver dagger with no special abilities. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spiral Athame

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs=""
>
[Shadewither Key](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1818)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This palm-sized talisman hangs on a string of braided bark fibers. It resembles a leaf from some unknown tree similar to oak, carved from jade and then dipped halfway into black ink that looks wet despite feeling completely dry. The gate key was originally created to open the aiudara known as the _Seventh Arch_. In recent years, corrupting magic has given it additional, stranger powers.

 When you wear and invest _the Shadewither Key_, shadows gather around you. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. However, this boon comes with great risk: any [elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) who touches _the Shadewither Key_ is immediately afflicted with the [_obnubilate curse_](/Curses.aspx?ID=49).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You touch _the Shadewither Key_ and command the shadows it casts around you to peel away. The shadows form an umbral double that resembles you in every way except for its fiery red eyes and the expression of wicked delight on its face. The double occupies the same space as you and attempts to intercept any attacks aimed at you. A creature must succeed at a DC 11 flat check when targeting you with an attack, spell, or other effect; on a failure, they hit the shadowy double instead of you. The effect lasts for 1 minute or until the double is hit, whichever comes first. Once the double is hit, it's destroyed. As long as the double exists, you don't gain _the Shadewither Key's_ item bonus to Stealth checks.

**Destruction** _The Shadewither Key's_ magic can be destroyed by exposing the artifact to uncontained teleportation energies. If touched to an unlinked or malfunctioning _aiudara_, _the Shadewither Key_ causes the gate to connect to a new counterpart. If this happens, the key's magic leaves it, rendering the item useless.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadewither Key

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs=""
>
[Stalk Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1821)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These black leather goggle frames have no lenses. Instead, when a character puts them on and invests them, the wearer's eyes transform and lengthen into snail-like eyestalks. The wobbly eyestalks stretch out through the lens holes and up over the wearer's head on lengthened optic nerves.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** By focusing hard, you can watch for enemies in all directions. You gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute; during this time, you can't be flanked.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Stalk Goggles

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs=""
>
Stalk Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 80

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate the goggles, you also gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight for the duration of the effect.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs=""
>
Stalk Goggles (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 80

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate the goggles, you also gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight and [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416) for the duration of the effect.
name: Stalk Goggles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bottled Omen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1822)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 81

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This potion holds a tiny, tightly wrapped scroll and tastes like paper. Upon drinking it, you gain a burst of insight into your immediate future—and how to potentially avoid it. When you attempt a saving throw, you can roll twice and use the better result. The potion's magic ends when you make use of this effect, or after 1 minute. You then become immune to _bottled omen_ potions for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bottled Omen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Draft of Stellar Radiance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1823)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #187: The Seventh Arch](/Sources.aspx?ID=192) pg. 81

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This potion's bottle glows softly with shimmering silver light. Upon drinking this potion, you're surrounded by a nimbus of blazing starlight that lasts for 1 minute. You emanate a field of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) with a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for another 20 feet, like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60)). You take a –20 penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. Any creature that targets you with an attack or an ability must succeed at a DC 17 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round. A creature who succeeds at this save is immune to the effect for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Draft of Stellar Radiance

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Armor Latches](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1824)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 11, [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 258

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This armor is easily doffed. A set of armor with armor latches gains the [noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=205) trait; you can't add latches to armor that already possesses the noisy trait. You can remove a set of armor with armor latches with a 3-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. This doesn't affect the [time it takes to don the armor](/Rules.aspx?ID=200).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Armor Latches

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Storage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1825)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 11

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to armor or unarmored defense clothing

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The storage adjustment fits the armor or clothing with belts, buckles, pouches, and loops for holding and storing tools. Countless fasteners make the armor as jangly as chain mail. While wearing armor with this adjustment, you can [wear](/Rules.aspx?ID=1456) up to 3 Bulk of tools instead of the usual 2. However, the armor acquires the [noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=205) trait. If it already has the noisy trait, increase its penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Storage

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1826)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 11

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A suit of armor with this adjustment incorporates short, flexible harnesses meant to connect weapons to each of its vambraces. These harnesses can each be connected to a melee weapon of light Bulk or less. Attaching or removing a weapon takes an Interact action. Someone else can attach or remove a weapon if you're willing to let them or you're unable to act. You must remove a weapon from its mount before you can completely [Release](/Actions.aspx?ID=83) or otherwise stow it.

 You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC against attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) you of a weapon connected to the armor. If the weapon would be knocked from your grasp or you would drop it, the weapon dangles from the bracer by its harness rather than falling to the ground. You can regain control of the weapon in the normal time it takes you to draw it. Attaching a weapon to a weapon harness prevents you from throwing it or using other abilities that would require it to leave your person.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Weapon Harness

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Assisting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1829)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 12

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your armor provides support for your joints or advanced prostheses for missing limbs, holding your body in place and easing physical symptoms. This replicates the benefits of any number of [splints, supports, and prostheses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2213). When you invest the armor, you determine how many such supports you want, and where on your body they assist you.

 In addition, the extra support and structure provided by the armor allows you to transport more than you would otherwise normally be able to. You can carry Bulk equal to 6 + your Strength modifier before becoming encumbered, and you can hold and carry a total Bulk of up to 11 + your Strength modifier.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Assisting

A complex pattern of protective symbols gives protection against various forms of energy, but only part of the runic structure can be active at a given time.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You take acid, cold, electricity, or fire damage;  **Effect** You gain resistance 5 to the triggering damage type. This doesn't apply to the triggering damage. This resistance lasts until you Activate this rune again or the armor is no longer invested by you.

</column>

---

The armor allows you to make a controlled descent.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You glide slowly toward the ground, 5 feet down and up to 25 feet forward through the air. Provided you spend at least 1 action gliding on your turn and haven't yet reached the ground, you remain in the air at the end of your turn. Otherwise, you fall.

>
[Immovable](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1832)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 12

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune utilizes magical principles to manipulate gravity and turn you into an immovable object.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your armor anchors you in place, even defying gravity, rendering you [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until you Dismiss the Activation. while you're immobilized in this way, you can be moved only if a creature succeeds at a DC 40 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) your armor. You can also be moved if 8,000 pounds of pressure are placed upon you, though this is likely fatal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immovable

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetizing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1833)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 13

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto metal armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune alters the magnetic polarity of your armor, making other metal items drawn to it ever so slightly. You can amplify the magnetic power of the armor to keep another creature from getting away from you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** A creature adjacent to you is made of metal or wearing metal armor; **Effect** You magnetize your armor. If you or the target attempt to move away from one another, treat each square as difficult terrain. This doesn't affect movement that keeps you the same distance from one another, so you could still Step if you remained adjacent to the target. This effect ends once either of you is no longer adjacent to the other at the end of an action.

 This Activation might work on some creatures partially made of metal, such as those with metal scales, as determined by the GM. Likewise, the GM might determine some metal creatures are non-magnetic, depending on the metal they're made of.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magnetizing

This rune allows your armor to collapse into a disguised, portable form.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You doff your armor, which folds into another wearable object, such as a bangle or amulet with light Bulk. This wearable object has features that hint at the armor it hides. You aren't wearing the armor while it's in this form, but at the GM's discretion, you can still activate properties that might feasibly come from the wearable item the armor has become. If the armor is in its portable form, you can use this activation to revert it to armor, which you can don in 1 minute.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spellwatch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1837)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 13

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Counter-runes chip away at unwanted magic that impedes you. You can attempt a new saving throw against one [hostile](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) spell affecting you at the start of each of your turns. If you succeed, the spell ends, and if you fail it continues unchanged, with no additional ill effect. If you’re affected by multiple hostile spells, choose only one each turn to attempt the additional save against.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spellwatch

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swallow-Spike](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1838)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 13

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Attacks made by armor with the swallow-spike rune apply the Multiple Attack Penalty as if you had made them with another weapon._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your armor responds to your desire to break free of a creature grabbing you by growing spikes.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), or otherwise held immobilized in a creature's grasp, such as by being [engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14) or [swallowed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35);  **Effect** Your armor suddenly grows spikes, attacking the triggering creature. The armor makes a melee attack with an attack modifier of +14 that deals 2d6 piercing damage. If the creature is swallowing or engulfing you, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage, and damage from this attack can cut you free if it equals or exceeds the Rupture value of the immobilizing ability. This attack gets an item bonus to the attack roll equal to the armor's item bonus to your AC and an item bonus to damage equal to double that amount.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** You're being held immobilized as described in the rune's other activation; **Effect** Your armor attacks the creature immobilizing you. The armor makes a melee attack against the creature, as described in the rune's other activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Swallow-Spike
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 13

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Swallow-Spike (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 13

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The attack modifier increases to +22, the damage increases to 3d6, and the extra damage to an engulfing or swallowing creature increases to 2d6.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Swallow-Spike (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 13

**Price** 19,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The attack modifier increases to +28, the damage increases to 5d6, and the extra damage to an engulfing or swallowing creature increases to 3d6.
name: Swallow-Spike

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arachnid Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1839)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 14

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_ has four knobbed ribs that wrap around the torso. The armor grants you resistance 2 to poison damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ribs from the armor spread into functional spider limbs. For 1 minute, you gain a climb Speed equal to half your land Speed and have your limbs free as you climb. If you use all your free limbs to help you Climb, your climb Speed equals your land Speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Arachnid Harness
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include four intact legs from a [giant tarantula](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3208).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Arachnid Harness (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 14

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) leather armor_, the resistance is 5, and the harness can be activated once every 10 minutes.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include four intact legs from a [goliath spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3209).
name: Arachnid Harness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Autumn's Embrace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1840)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 14

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leaf Weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Woven by [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) seamstresses as rewards for servants of nature, countless leaves continually changing colors in autumnal hues comprise autumn's embrace, a suit of _+2 [invisibility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=287) [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leaf weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)_. Leaves shed from the armor as they might fall in autumn. When activating the armor's _invisibility_ property rune, you disappear in a swirl of colorful leaves.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call forth a storm of leaves from _autumn's embrace_. These leaves swirl in a 20-foot emanation for 1 minute. Creatures within the area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and creatures outside the area are concealed to creatures within the leaves. However, you can see through this concealment. You can Dismiss the activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Autumn's Embrace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bastion of the Inheritor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1841)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 14

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Bastion Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Worn by [Iomedae's](/Deities.aspx?ID=9) prestigious knights, this _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [bastion plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)_ is emblazoned with the Inheritor's [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) and sports a white cloak. While the cloak is white, this armor grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), provided your target has no enmity toward Iomedae.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The armor's cloak becomes red for 1 minute. As long as the cloak is red, you gain the benefits of the armor's deflect melee trait without needing to spend an additional action to activate it during each turn.

**Craft Requirements** You worship Iomedae.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bastion of the Inheritor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black Hole Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1842)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 14

**Price** 8,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Fortress Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The joints of this black _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [fortress plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21)_ look like swirling vortices of silver. Non-magical ammunition and thrown weapons aimed at you are destroyed after they hit you and deal damage or miss you. You also have resistance 10 to physical damage from ranged attacks.

 When an enemy's ranged attack misses you or hits you and deals no damage, the armor absorbs the projectile. When it has absorbed six projectiles, the armor glows at the joints.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** A ranged weapon Strike targets a creature within 20 feet of you and the attacker hasn't yet rolled its attack;  **Effect** The triggering Strike targets you instead of its intended target.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** The _black hole armor_ has absorbed six or more projectiles; **Effect** All the projectiles absorbed by the armor appear out of thin air, as though transported there, falling in a 10-foot burst within 120 feet of you. Each creature in the burst takes 10d8 piercing damage with a DC 35 Reflex save. The absorbed projectiles are all expended, and the armor's joints stop glowing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Black Hole Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade Byrnie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1843)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 15

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Instead of chain links, this _+1 [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)_ is assembled from metal “leaves” that each resemble a small blade.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You pull a link from the armor, which transforms into a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_. The dagger disappears and reappears as a link in the armor after you Strike with it, or at the end of this turn if you don't make a Strike. You can Activate the blade byrnie in place of an Interact action to draw a weapon for abilities such as the [Quick Draw](/Feats.aspx?ID=498) feat.

 Upgrading the runes on the _blade byrnie_ makes the daggers pulled from it more powerful. The daggers have a [_+2 weapon potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=281) rune if the armor has a [_+2 armor potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=278) rune, or a _+3 weapon potency_ rune if the armor has a _+3 armor potency_ rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 7"

>

</title>

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is a _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) chain shirt_. The daggers are _+2 greater striking daggers_.

level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blade Byrnie (Major)
</title>
