<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade Byrnie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1843)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 15

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Instead of chain links, this _+1 [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)_ is assembled from metal “leaves” that each resemble a small blade.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You pull a link from the armor, which transforms into a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_. The dagger disappears and reappears as a link in the armor after you Strike with it, or at the end of this turn if you don't make a Strike. You can Activate the blade byrnie in place of an Interact action to draw a weapon for abilities such as the [Quick Draw](/Feats.aspx?ID=498) feat.

 Upgrading the runes on the _blade byrnie_ makes the daggers pulled from it more powerful. The daggers have a [_+2 weapon potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=281) rune if the armor has a [_+2 armor potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=278) rune, or a _+3 weapon potency_ rune if the armor has a _+3 armor potency_ rune.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Devil's Bargain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1844)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 15

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Those favored by [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2) can be found wearing this _+1 [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_, which is lacquered in red and black.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically fail an attack roll, check, or saving throw;  **Effect** You offer a prayer to Asmodeus, who accepts under a reciprocal condition. Reroll the triggering roll with a +2 circumstance bonus and use the higher result.

 If this reroll improves your degree of success, the GM can later reroll one Strike against you, one saving throw against your abilities, or one [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) check or saving throw you make. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect if used on a Strike or save against you, or a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect if used on your secret check or saving throw. The GM must use this reroll before the end of the last day you activated the armor.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Devil's Bargain

    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Turtle Plate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1845)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 15

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This harness of _[+1 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [ half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=49)_ is made from the shell plates of a [dragon turtle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2956) and has the [aquadynamic](/Traits.aspx?ID=792) trait. To those who can craft with them, the dense organic plates on the dragon turtle’s body are as valuable as the creature’s hoard of gold.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** A creature adjacent to you targets you with a melee attack;  **Effect** You roll the hefty plates of the dragon turtle armor toward the attacker to gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the shell of a dragon turtle.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Turtle Plate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Energizing Lattice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1846)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 15

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Lattice Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=27)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [lattice armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=27)_ has latticework of fine golden wire. After negating a critical hit with its _fortification_ rune, the latticework glows for 1 minute, shedding [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet). You can Dismiss this light.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610))   **Requirements** The _energizing lattice_ is glowing because it negated an enemy's critical hit; **Effect** You release the lattice's energy and make a weapon or unarmed attack Strike that deals 6d6 additional force damage. If your Strike fails, but doesn't critically fail, the target still takes half the force damage. The latticework ceases glowing after the Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Energizing Lattice

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Immortal Bastion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1847)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 15

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Bastion Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This impressive _+3 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [greater fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [bastion plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)_ is built like an impregnable castle, with multiple layers of defense and no weak points. When you activate the armor's deflect melee trait, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against melee attacks instead of +1, and you gain 10 temporary Hit Points that last until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are reduced to 0 Hit Points or would die from a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect;  **Effect** You drop to 1 Hit Point instead of being reduced to 0 HP or dying, and you gain 100 temporary Hit Points that last until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would gain or increase the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) or [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition;  **Effect** You avoid gaining or increasing the condition. If the triggering effect imposes both doomed and wounded, choose only one to prevent. This doesn't remove either of the conditions if you already have them, nor does it prevent the same triggering effect from giving or increasing the prevented condition later.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immortal Bastion

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

These _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [scroll robes](/Armor.aspx?ID=35)_ magically store a spell for you. During your daily preparations, choose one spell you know of 5th level or lower. You inscribe that spell on the robes, as though you had done so using the robes' [inscribed](/Traits.aspx?ID=480) trait, but without needing to go through the normal scribing process. You must provide the minimum amount of materials to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) one scroll of that spell (typically half the Price of a scroll of that level plus any extra cost required for the spell). You don't need to be trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), nor do you need the [Magical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=812) feat. Using this ability erases any scroll already inscribed on the robe.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Linnorm's Sankeit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1849)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 18

**Price** 22,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=34)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The first suit of _+3 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [antimagic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=282) [sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=34)_ made in this fashion was crafted for the Varki linnorm king Nankou, after he claimed his title by slaying a [taiga linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=723). By Varki tradition, the armor was crafted using some of the slain linnorm's body to decorate the breastplate and helm, which imbued the armor with several of the linnorm's natural abilities. Though the helm is shaped like the beast's head, a linnorm's actual head would be too large for a proper helmet.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You breathe a 60-foot cone of electrified vapor, dealing 17d6 electricity damage to creatures in the area (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The electrified mist persists in the area for 2 rounds, dealing 5d6 electricity damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to each creature that ends its turn in the mist. If you slew the linnorm this armor is made from, you can use the higher of your class DC or spell DC instead of the listed DCs for this effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** A creature adjacent to you targets you with a melee attack;  **Effect** The creature takes 4d6 piercing damage as previously invisible magical spines leap outwards from the armor to punish them for the attack.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide and skull of any type of [linnorm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=439).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Linnorm's Sankeit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ouroboros Buckles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1850)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 18

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Buckle Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=18)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ominous-looking _+3 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [greater acid-resistant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=283) [buckle armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=18)_ is deep red in color, favored by assassins who worship [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=13) or [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2), and if unfastened, the many belts and buckles writhe like living snakes. _Ouroboros buckles_ have the [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=203) trait. However, while wearing _ouroboros buckles_, the unfathomable concept of infinity pulls at your mind, and each time you awaken, you'd swear the armor hissed into your ear while you were asleep. The hissing suggested secret wisdom to you in [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), though you only ever remember it vaguely, like a fading dream.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call forth the cursed regenerative blood of an [ouroboros](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1256) to recover from your wounds. You gain [regeneration 15](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=26) for 1 minute; [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) damage deactivates this regeneration. Each time you regain Hit Points from this regeneration, if you're in your normal form, you collapse into a large swarm of Tiny snakes. This is a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effect that changes you into a battle form.

 When you're a swarm of snakes, you gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) (30 feet), a Speed of 20 feet, a climb Speed of 20 feet, and a swim Speed of 20 feet. You have an AC of 16 + your level and ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction. Also, you gain resistance 5 to physical damage and weakness 5 to area damage and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. You can use none of your normal Strikes. Instead, you can use Swarming Bites, a single action that deals 4d4 piercing damage plus 2d6 poison damage to creatures in your space with a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

 If you're in this battle form and are already at full HP when your regeneration occurs, the snakes instantaneously slither together, and you transform into your original form.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** A foe within 15 feet hits you with a Strike that deals slashing or piercing damage;  **Effect** Your _ouroboros buckles_ spray acidic blood in a 15-foot cone toward the foe, dealing 5d6 acid damage with a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ouroboros Buckles

<title

    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Reef Heart](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1851)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 18

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Coral Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=20)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [coral armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=20)_, often favored by allies of [merfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=73) and aquatic [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2), is made of living coral carefully harvested from the ocean depths. Legends speak of a variety of similar coral armors with distinct powers, but surface-dwellers know of _reef heart_ as a magic armor that makes it easier for them to travel under the sea. _Reef heart_ enables you to breathe underwater and gives you a swim Speed equal to half your land Speed.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_coral eruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=887). If you conjure the coral underwater, it ceases dealing damage after 1 minute, but its duration is unlimited. If the magical coral remains in place for 1 year, it becomes non-magical coral from which a reef might grow.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

The reef heart is _+2 greater resilient coral armor_, the swim Speed is equal to your full land Speed, and the _coral eruption_ is 7th level.

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [coral armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=20)_, often favored by allies of [merfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=73) and aquatic [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2), is made of living coral carefully harvested from the ocean depths. Legends speak of a variety of similar coral armors with distinct powers, but surface-dwellers know of _reef heart_ as a magic armor that makes it easier for them to travel under the sea. _Reef heart_ enables you to breathe underwater and gives you a swim Speed equal to half your land Speed.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_coral eruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=887). If you conjure the coral underwater, it ceases dealing damage after 1 minute, but its duration is unlimited. If the magical coral remains in place for 1 year, it becomes non-magical coral from which a reef might grow.

</column>

As you fight, the armor glows red hot.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor casts [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=118) on you.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the carapace of a [firewyrm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2983).

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 19

**Price** 476 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Lamellar](/Armor.aspx?ID=29)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [_+1_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [_leather lamellar_](/Armor.aspx?ID=29) _armor_ incorporates the plates of an [ore louse’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2665) hide, woven together with the creature’s own sinew into a functional set of armor. While wearing the _rusting carapace_, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against any effect that would rust you or your items, such as the ore louse’s Strikes that have the rust ability or the [_rust cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1377) spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Oxidizing liquid seeps from between the segments of the rusting hide onto a nearby metal creature, non-magical metal hazard, or non-magical metal item of up to 1 Bulk. A targeted creature or hazard gets a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save; a targeted item automatically gets a critical failure if unattended, but a creature holding or wearing it can attempt a DC 24 basic Reflex save. The rust deals 8d6 damage to a creature or hazard, plus 2d6 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) on a failed save; it deals 2d6 damage to an item, plus 1d6 persistent damage on a failed save.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide of a [ore louse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2665).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rusting Carapace

This suit of _+1 [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7)_ is sickly green and covered in thick warts and nodules, fashioned from the hide of a [trollhound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=835) and imbued with some of the beast's vitality. Wearing this armor gives you a –1 circumstance penalty to all checks made using [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to interact with [trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=395) and a +1 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks used to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) in communities traditionally plagued by troll attacks.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** You take damage from a melee attack while you have half or fewer of your normal maximum Hit Points;  **Effect** Your body knits itself back together, healing you for 3d8 Hit Points.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hides of at least two [trollhounds](/Monsters.aspx?ID=835).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wasp Guard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1855)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 19

**Price** 487 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

[Druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=6) and [Calistria's](/Deities.aspx?ID=3) faithful alike value this vindictive armor for its ability to turn away pests and exact revenge on foes in a manner that can easily turn coordinated enemies into a chaotic mass. _Wasp guard_ is _+1 [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_ studded with the carapaces of [wasps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=99), subtly diverting swarms away and granting a +1 status bonus to Reflex saving throws against Swarming Bites, Swarming Stings, and similar abilities from [swarms](/Traits.aspx?ID=239).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy within 30 feet Strikes you with an attack;  **Effect** You animate the exoskeletons of the _wasp guard_ armor into a spectral swarm of wasps that seeks revenge for the slight. The swarm flies to the triggering enemy's space and swarms about them, dealing 1d8 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). If the affected enemy has any allies within 30 feet, the enemy can spend a single action, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, to shoo the swarm away. This ends the persistent damage on that enemy automatically, but the swarm instead moves to affect the enemy's ally.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 1 Bulk of wasp exoskeletons.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wasp Guard

<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />

[Barbarians](/Classes.aspx?ID=2), [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=6) and other outlanders are often forced to spend the harsh winter months protecting their communities from one of the deadliest predators to stalk the forests and taiga of the northern reaches, the fearsome [witchwargs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3231). This _+1 [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7)_ is assembled from the hide and fur of a trio of witchwargs, and it conveys on the wearer both an attack akin to the witchwargs' deadly jaws and the ability to channel the frigid cold of the witchwargs's Winter Breath.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You breathe a cloud of frost in a 15-foot cone that deals 5d8 cold damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain a jaws unarmed attack that you make using your hands. This attack deals 1d8 piercing damage plus 1 cold damage, is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and has the [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hides of at least three [witchwargs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3231).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This [standard-grade duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2815) [meteor shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=13) (Hardness 7, HP 28, BT 14) is a wheel from a ship. While wielding the shield, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You're in a body of water; **Effect** For 10 minutes, you don't sink if you haven't succeeded at a Swim action on a turn, and if you're submerged, you automatically ascend 10 feet at the end of your turn.
